content: The size of the universe can be quantified using methods such as measuring the cosmic microwave background radiation, observing the redshift of distant galaxies, and employing standard candles like Type Ia supernovae to determine distances. The observable universe is estimated to be about 93 billion light-years in diameter, but the entire universe may be much larger or even infinite.
raw_content: The size of the universe can be quantified using methods such as measuring the cosmic microwave background radiation, observing the redshift of distant galaxies, and employing standard candles like Type Ia supernovae to determine distances. The observable universe is estimated to be about 93 billion light-years in diameter, but the entire universe may be much larger or even infinite.
